mcqs.question: What is the main difference between a Turing Machine and a Finite Automaton?

options.text: A Turing Machine has a finite tape, while a Finite Automaton has an infinite tape
A Turing Machine has a read/write head, while a Finite Automaton has only a read head
A Turing Machine can simulate the behavior of any algorithm, while a Finite Automaton cannot
A Turing Machine is simpler than a Finite Automaton

mcqs.explanation: A Turing Machine is more powerful than a Finite Automaton because it can simulate the behavior of any algorithm.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the transition function in a Turing Machine?

options.text: To determine the next state of the machine
To determine the next action of the machine
To determine both the next state and action of the machine
To determine the initial state of the machine

mcqs.explanation: The transition function determines both the next state and action of the machine based on the current state and symbol read.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between a deterministic and non-deterministic Turing Machine?

options.text: A deterministic Turing Machine always accepts a given input string, while a non-deterministic Turing Machine may reject it
A deterministic Turing Machine has a single possible next state, while a non-deterministic Turing Machine has multiple possible next states
A deterministic Turing Machine is simpler than a non-deterministic Turing Machine
A deterministic Turing Machine is more powerful than a non-deterministic Turing Machine

mcqs.explanation: A deterministic Turing Machine has a single possible next state, while a non-deterministic Turing Machine has multiple possible next states.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the halting state in a Turing Machine?

options.text: To indicate that the machine has accepted the input string
To indicate that the machine has rejected the input string
To indicate that the machine has finished processing the input string
To indicate that the machine has encountered an error

mcqs.explanation: The halting state indicates that the machine has finished processing the input string.

mcqs.question: What is the relationship between the Turing Machine and the Church-Turing thesis?

options.text: The Turing Machine is a proof of the Church-Turing thesis
The Turing Machine is a counterexample to the Church-Turing thesis
The Turing Machine is equivalent to the Church-Turing thesis
The Turing Machine is a special case of the Church-Turing thesis

mcqs.explanation: The Turing Machine is equivalent to the Church-Turing thesis, which states that any effectively calculable function can be computed by a Turing Machine.
